raw_response:
{
  "ai_system": {
    "name": "Guided Search - Personalized Search Refinements",
    "description": "A machine learning model that suggests the most relevant search refinements to users based on their current filter selections and the region they are searching in. The model uses clickstream data to predict which filters users are likely to apply next.",
    "functionality": [
      "Analyzes user search behavior and regional patterns to suggest relevant filters",
      "Ranks refinement suggestions based on probability of user engagement",
      "Adapts to regional differences in user preferences",
      "Considers the context of already applied filters when making suggestions",
      "Implements two approaches for filter prediction: co-occurrence and sequential filter changes"
    ],
    "data_sources": [
      "User clickstream data from Zillow searches",
      "Filter change events",
      "Regional information",
      "Current set of applied filters"
    ],
    "model_approach": {
      "type": "Probabilistic ranking model",
      "description": "Estimates the probability P(fi | F, g) that a user will click on a refinement suggestion fi given the current set of applied filters F and the search region g. Uses two approaches: co-occurrence of filters and sequential filter changes."
    },
    "results": {
      "regional_insights": "Captures regional differences in filter preferences (e.g., Pool and AC in hotter cities, pet preferences, on-site parking in dense cities)",
      "contextual_ranking": "Adjusts filter rankings based on already selected filters (e.g., different numeric filter values for different bedroom counts)",
      "ab_test_results": "33% lift in engagement with filter suggestions, positive impact on business metrics (saved homes, conversions)"
    },
    "future_improvements": [
      "Expand to include more home attributes beyond structured filters",
      "Extract and rank unstructured attributes from home descriptions",
      "Use user interactions with suggestions as training data for statistical learning to rank models"
    ],
    "impact": {
      "user_experience": "Improves the Zillow search experience by providing more relevant filter suggestions",
      "business_metrics": "Positive impact on engagement, home saves, and conversions"
    }
  },
  "deployment_status": {
    "current_status": "Currently running an A/B test in select regions",
    "next_steps": "Plan to expand the model to more regions and improve upon current results"
  },
  "related_work": [
    "Predicting sparse down-funnel events with transfer and multi-target learning",
    "Beyond Clicks: Designing AI-Driven User Memory for Personalization",
    "Revolutionizing the Real Estate Experience with LLMs: StreetEasy’s AI Journey"
  ],
  "team": [
    "Adam Cohn",
    "Andrei Lopatenko",
    "Arun Balagopalan",
    "Elysia Chu",
    "Farah Abdallah Akoum",
    "Kevin Zhao",
    "Marie Laura-Luisada",
    "Zehua Zhou"
  ],
  "acknowledgements": [
    "Andrei Lopatenko",
    "Arun Balagopalan",
    "Farah Abdallah Akoum",
    "Xin Jin"
  ]
}

raw_response:
{
  "ai_system": {
    "name": "Faire Real-Time Ranking System",
    "description": "A consolidated real-time ranking system designed to provide high-quality, granular, and personalized ranking scores for search and recommendation surfaces at Faire.",
    "components": [
      {
        "name": "Retrieval",
        "description": "Uses ElasticSearch to fetch a smaller candidate pool of products for re-ranking. ElasticSearch determines relevance based on a weighted scoring function using BM25 for search, and reformulates queries for category navigation and brand pages.",
        "features": [
          "BM25-based relevance scoring for search",
          "Top products by quality score for category navigation",
          "All products in a specified brand's catalog for brand pages",
          "Embedding-based retrieval for recommendation surfaces"
        ]
      },
      {
        "name": "Feature Store",
        "description": "A system that uses both offline batch-computed and online real-time computed features to evaluate models on the fly. Features and embeddings are stored in Redis for low-latency retrieval.",
        "features": [
          "Offline feature computation using SQL queries and ML models",
          "Online feature computation using a main backend service",
          "Embeddings for complex characteristics like retailer order history and brand product catalogs",
          "Caching of recent retailer activity for real-time personalization"
        ]
      },
      {
        "name": "Offline Learning",
        "description": "A 'log and wait' approach where all retrieved and computed features are logged and streamed into the data warehouse for model training. Ensures data fidelity between training and inference time.",
        "features": [
          "XGBoost models trained on add-to-carts using a pointwise loss function",
          "Logging of features for every item shown to a retailer",
          "ETL pipelines to combine logged features with training labels",
          "Periodic retraining of models using logged features"
        ]
      },
      {
        "name": "Model Store",
        "description": "Models are retrained periodically and uploaded to an AWS S3 bucket with all associated model artifacts. The system is integrated into an internal experimentation framework for easy deployment.",
        "features": [
          "XGBoost models for current version",
          "Future support for neural network models",
          "Good null handling and tolerance for numeric feature distributions",
          "Minimal code required to deploy new models"
        ]
      },
      {
        "name": "Model Evaluation in Re-ranking",
        "description": "Scores each product using pre-trained models and re-ranks the candidate set. A secondary ranker is used to solve specific ranking challenges.",
        "features": [
          "XGBoost models for inference speed and tooling maturity",
          "Re-ranking of all products in the candidate set",
          "Future support for neural network models",
          "Secondary ranker for solving specific ranking challenges"
        ]
      }
    ],
    "requirements": [
      "Latency: Server side request/response latency budget of 500ms",
      "Extensibility: Support for multiple item types (products, brands, carousels)",
      "Developer Experience: Easy to plug into existing surfaces and extend with new ML features",
      "Data Fidelity: Ensure data used in offline training is computed the same way as in production",
      "Monitoring and Alerting: Ability to monitor system health and build monitoring and alerting"
    ],
    "key_features": [
      "Embedding-based retrieval for recommendation surfaces",
      "Offline and online feature computation",
      "XGBoost models for re-ranking",
      "Feature store with Redis for low-latency retrieval",
      "Log and wait approach for data fidelity",
      "Model store with AWS S3 for model artifacts",
      "Secondary ranker for specific ranking challenges"
    ],
    "benefits": [
      "High-quality, granular, and personalized ranking scores",
      "Scalability for future growth",
      "Rapid iterations on ranking models",
      "Minimized toil associated with wrangling training data",
      "Easy deployment of new models with minimal code changes"
    ]
  }
}

  "ai_technology_impact": "The use of LLMs and RAG enables the system to handle sparse data and infer preferences from restaurant order history, while prompt engineering ensures consistent and high-quality outputs.     "Enables cold-start recommendations by inferring preferences from restaurant order data",

  ],
  "ai_technology_challenges": [
    "Managing context size to prevent hallucinations",
    "Ensuring consistent output formats through prompt engineering",
    "Balancing recency and frequency in tagset scoring",

  ],

  "ai_system": {
    "name": "Imputation Model Evaluation System",
    "description": "A system for evaluating and comparing the performance of various imputation models, particularly autoencoder variants, for the purpose of improving data quality in the Zestimate pipeline.",
    "purpose": "To assess the effectiveness of different imputation techniques in reconstructing missing or corrupted home attribute data, with the goal of improving the accuracy of property value estimates.",
    "key_components": [
      "Autoencoder architectures (Denoising, Variational)",
      "Feature engineering and data preprocessing",
      "Custom evaluation metrics",
      "Model benchmarking against median imputation"
    ],
    "data_sources": [
      "Zillow's dataset of over 100 million homes",
      "Subsample of 1 million homes for training",
      "100 thousand homes for scoring"
    ],
    "model_types": [
      "Denoising Autoencoder (DAE)",
      "Variational Autoencoder (VAE)",
      "Random Forest",
      "k-Nearest Neighbors (kNN)",
      "Median Imputation (baseline)"
    ],
    "evaluation_metrics": [
      "Percentage of imputed values within a certain range of the true value",
      "Feature-specific metrics for build year, lot size, bedrooms, etc."
    ],
    "results": {
      "DAE_performance": "Outperformed median imputation for most features, particularly in build year predictions",
      "VAE_performance": "Similar to DAE in most cases",
      "Random_Forest_kNN_performance": "Comparable to autoencoders for some features but underperformed for others",
      "Lot_Size_Challenges": "All models struggled with lot size predictions, with DAE being the exception"
    },
    "conclusion": "Autoencoder models, particularly DAE and VAE, showed significant improvements over the median imputation baseline for most home attribute features. These models demonstrate potential for improving data quality in the Zestimate pipeline, though further refinement may be needed for specific features like lot size."
  }
}

raw_response:
{
  "doc_id": "DOC000252",
  "company": "eBay",
  "industry": "E-commerce and retail",
  "year": 2024,
  "title": "Cutting Through the Noise: Three Things We've Learned About Generative AI and Developer Productivity",
  "ai_use_cases": [
    {
      "name": "GitHub Copilot Integration",
      "description": "eBay expanded the use of GitHub Copilot to all developers, conducting an A/B test with 300 developers. The results showed increased perceived productivity, a 27% code acceptance rate, and improvements in PR merge time and lead time for change.",
      "impact": "Positive impact on developer productivity, with measurable improvements in code generation and efficiency."
    },
    {
      "name": "eBayCoder (Fine-Tuned LLM)",
      "description": "eBay fine-tuned the Code Llama 13B model to create eBayCoder, which is trained on eBay's codebase and documentation. This model helps with software upkeep, reducing code duplication and improving task efficiency.",
      "impact": "Improved efficiency in software maintenance and reduced code duplication by leveraging internal data and context."
    },
    {
      "name": "Internal GPT for Knowledge Retrieval",
      "description": "eBay developed an internal GPT system using Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) to help developers find answers to common questions by querying internal documentation sources like GitHub Markdowns, Google Docs, Jira, Slack, and Wikis.",
      "impact": "Increased developer efficiency by reducing the time spent on investigations and improving access to internal knowledge."
    }
  ],
  "metrics_used": [
    "Code acceptance rate (27%)",
    "Code accuracy (70% for documents, 60% for code)",
    "PR creation to merge time (17% decrease)",
    "Lead Time for Change (12% decrease)",
    "Code quality (measured via Sonar, no change between groups)",
    "Task completion time",
    "Number of support and meeting requests"
  ],
  "challenges": [
    "GitHub Copilot's limited prompt size, which restricts its usefulness for large-scale tasks.",
    "Code duplication issues in large codebases, which require fine-tuned models to address.",
    "Nonsensical answers from internal GPT systems, which can be mitigated through Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF)."
  ],
  "conclusion": "eBay has identified three key tracks for leveraging generative AI to improve developer productivity: using commercial offerings like GitHub Copilot, fine-tuning open-source LLMs to create eBayCoder, and building an internal GPT system for knowledge retrieval. These approaches have shown measurable improvements in code generation, efficiency, and task completion, with the potential for exponential growth in productivity over time."
}

It uses tradecraft prompting to encode detailed threat modeling practices within prompts and prompt chains. The system maps system representations (e.g., textual descriptions or architecture diagrams) to threat models, capturing threat modeling tradecraft knowledge. The project includes contributions such as the design of the system, a technical formulation of the threat modeling task, and an initial evaluation based on subject matter expert feedback for real banking systems. The system is currently in the research phase and has not been deployed in production environments.",

{
  "title": "LLM-Powered Attribute Extraction for Enhanced Retail Catalog Quality at DoorDash",
  "summary": "DoorDash leverages large language models (LLMs) to build scalable and accurate attribute extraction pipelines for new verticals in its retail catalog. These models address cold-start problems in natural language processing, enabling proactive brand ingestion, organic product labeling, and generalized attribute extraction. The downstream impact includes improved personalized recommendations, entity resolution for sponsored ads, and enhanced customer shopping experiences. The team is also exploring multimodal LLMs and building a centralized model platform to democratize LLM use across the company.",
  "ai_usage": {
    "type": "Production",
    "description": "LLMs are used in production pipelines for brand extraction, organic product labeling, and generalized attribute extraction. These models are integrated into end-to-end workflows that include retrieval-augmented generation, in-context learning, and fine-tuning for scalable inference. The models are actively used to improve catalog quality and downstream ML systems."
  },
  "ai_models_used": [
    "OpenAI GPT-4",
    "OpenAI Embeddings",
    "Custom fine-tuned LLMs"
  ],
  "ai_techniques_used": [
    "Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)",
    "In-Context Learning",
    "Approximate Nearest Neighbors (ANN)",
    "Fine-tuning",
    "Prompt Engineering"
  ],
  "ai_impact": {
    "business_impact": "Improved catalog quality, enhanced personalized recommendations, increased sponsored ad reach, and better customer engagement through targeted item carousels. The use of LLMs has significantly accelerated the annotation process, reducing time from months to weeks.",
    "technical_impact": "Enabled scalable and accurate attribute extraction without requiring large labeled datasets. The models have improved the efficiency and accuracy of brand ingestion, organic labeling, and entity resolution. The team is also building foundational infrastructure to support multimodal LLMs and a centralized model platform."
  },
  "ai_challenges": [
    "Handling abstraction and abbreviations in text-based product descriptions",
    "Reducing hallucination in generated annotations",
    "Scaling LLM use across the organization",
    "Integrating multimodal LLMs for image-based attribute extraction"
  ],
  "ai_future_plans": [
    "Exploring multimodal LLMs for attribute extraction from product images",
    "Building foundational technologies to allow Dashers to take product photos for in-store attribute extraction",
    "Democratizing LLM use across DoorDash through a centralized model platform",
    "Continuing to refine and expand the use of LLMs in catalog quality and personalized recommendations"
  ],
  "ai_team": {
    "team_name": "New Verticals Machine Learning Team",
    "team_description": "The team is focused on catalog quality, personalized recommendations, and foundational ML technologies. They are actively working on integrating LLMs into production pipelines and building infrastructure to support scalable and accurate attribute extraction."
  },
  "ai_infrastructure": {
    "description": "The team is building foundational technologies and infrastructure to support the use of LLMs across DoorDash. This includes a centralized model platform that allows for prompt engineering, fine-tuning, and deployment of LLMs. The team is also exploring multimodal LLMs and building systems to process product images for attribute extraction."
  },

      "focus": "Catalog Quality and Personalized Recommendations"
    },
    {
      "name": "Sree Chaitanya Vadru",

      "focus": "Foundational ML Technologies and Recommender Systems"
    }
  ]
}

{
  "ai_model_used": "Qwen",
  "analysis": {
    "ai_used": true,
    "ai_usage_explanation": "The text describes a machine learning system for job recommendation on ZipRecruiter. It discusses the use of pre-trained encoders, siamese networks, contrastive loss, focal loss, and triplet loss to learn semantic similarity between job postings and job seekers' resumes. These are all advanced AI/ML techniques used to train models to understand and represent the meaning of unstructured text data.",
    "ai_impact_on_analysis": "The AI techniques described in the text are central to the analysis. The entire system is built around AI/ML models to solve the problem of job recommendation in a multi-sided marketplace."
  },
  "main_points": [
    "ZipRecruiter has developed a machine learning system for job recommendation that addresses the challenges of a multi-sided marketplace.",
    "The system uses pre-trained encoders to learn semantic similarity between job postings and job seekers' resumes.",
    "A siamese network with contrastive focal loss is used to model job similarity based on co-applications by the same job seeker.",
    "A triplet loss function is used for the resume encoder to learn similarity between job seekers based on employer feedback.",
    "The system addresses the challenge of balancing competing stakeholder objectives (job seekers and employers) through a self-regularized dual loss function.",
    "The approach handles the complexities of unstructured text data, including non-standard formats, creative titles, and domain-specific jargon.",
    "The system has zero-shot capabilities, meaning it can accurately represent new entities in the online environment without explicit training on them.",
    "The ultimate goal is to predict which jobs are most suited for a given job seeker by inferring cross-entity similarity."
  ],
  "conclusion": "The text describes a sophisticated AI/ML system for job recommendation that addresses the unique challenges of a multi-sided marketplace. The system uses advanced techniques like pre-trained encoders, siamese networks, and dual loss functions to learn semantic similarity between job postings and job seekers' resumes. This enables the system to provide more accurate and balanced job recommendations while handling the complexities of unstructured text data."
}

raw_response:
{
  "system": {
    "name": "DoorDash Forecasting and Optimization System",
    "purpose": "To predict and manage supply and demand imbalances in the delivery market using machine learning and optimization techniques",
    "components": [
      {
        "name": "Forecasting System",
        "description": "Uses LightGBM for multivariate regression to predict supply and demand at regional levels",
        "features": [
          "Support for multivariate forecasting",
          "Extrapolation capabilities for new markets",
          "Counterfactual analysis support",
          "Minimal dependency footprint",
          "Thriving open-source community"
        ],
        "challenges": [
          "Understanding causality in data",
          "Matching forecasting units with decision-making units",
          "Avoiding overfitting to noisy data"
        ]
      },
      {
        "name": "Optimization System",
        "description": "Uses Mixed-Integer Programming (MIP) to allocate incentives optimally",
        "features": [
          "Custom objective function to minimize undersupply",
          "Flexible constraint system",
          "Budget constraints enforcement",
          "Uncertainty handling through resampling"
        ],
        "challenges": [
          "Dealing with forecast uncertainty",
          "Avoiding over-allocation in high-variance regions"
        ]
      }
    ],
    "data_flow": [
      "Collect historical data on supply, demand, and incentives",
      "Use LightGBM to train forecasting models",
      "Generate counterfactual scenarios for decision support",
      "Use forecasts as inputs to the MIP optimizer",
      "Apply resampling techniques to handle uncertainty",
      "Generate incentive allocation plans",
      "Monitor and update models based on new data"
    ],
    "key_concepts": [
      "Causal inference in forecasting",
      "Multivariate regression",
      "Mixed-integer programming",
      "Resampling for uncertainty estimation",
      "Domain knowledge integration",
      "Scalable forecasting for thousands of regions"
    ]
  }
}
